items.title: OpenAI launches new Codex tools for white-collar work
items.raw_content: OpenAI released a set of six plug-ins aimed at specific jobs: data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Available from within the Codex app, each of the new tools bundles integrations, instructions, and context to allow Codex to approximate a specific job.

items.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos  to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
items.raw_content: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries — targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications where a cyberattack could affect 100 million people.

items.title: Microsoft’s first advanced reasoning AI is here
items.raw_content: Microsoft announced a bunch of new in-house AI models at Build 2026, including a new "flagship" model: MAI-Thinking-1. It's an ambitious step into model development for Microsoft, which introduced its initial in-house models last year - before then, it had relied on OpenAI's models. The two companies recently renegotiated their deal to loosen ties. According […]

items.title: Microsoft’s Project Solara is an OS for AI agent gadgets
items.raw_content: Microsoft just announced "Project Solara," a new OS designed for gadgets that run AI agents, at Build 2026. The company is calling it "a new platform built from the ground up to power agent-driven experiences." It's built on Android, not Windows. Microsoft demonstrated two concept Project Solara devices at Build today: Desk concept and badge […]

items.title: New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions
items.raw_content: Microsoft on Tuesday took the wraps off Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for spinning up AI evaluations.
items.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday took the wraps off Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for spinning up AI evaluations.

items.title: Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant
items.raw_content: Launched at Build, Microsoft Scout is a new AI assistant meant to bring the power and flexibility of OpenClaw into the Microsoft 365 system.
items.summary: Launched at Build, Microsoft Scout is a new AI assistant meant to bring the power and flexibility of OpenClaw into the Microsoft 365 system.

items.title: Trump signs narrower executive order on AI oversight after industry objections
items.raw_content: After industry objections, President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models.
items.summary: After industry objections, President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models.

items.title: Microsoft Build 2026: The 7 biggest announcements
items.raw_content: Microsoft just kicked off Build 2026 with a keynote from CEO Satya Nadella and other company leaders. As expected, it was filled with announcements, ranging from new Surface hardware to an always-on personal assistant and updates across Microsoft's in-house AI models. If you didn't watch the event live, you can catch up on all the […]

items.title: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
items.raw_content: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Tuesday creating a "voluntary framework" for AI companies to share their frontier models with the federal government before they're released "to promote secure innovation and strengthen the cybersecurity of critical infrastructure." The order says the US AI industry has succeeded in part "because we refuse to stifle this […]

items.title: Microsoft Scout is a new AI personal assistant built on OpenClaw
items.raw_content: Much like Google, Microsoft is launching its own version of OpenClaw. Microsoft Scout is an always-on assistant that integrates into Microsoft 365 apps like Outlook, OneDrive, and Microsoft Teams, allowing businesses to assign a virtual assistant to employees to help with organizing calendars, expense reporting, email drafts, and much more. Unlike Copilot that lives inside […]

items.title: Google’s Phone app will tell you if a scammer is impersonating one of your contacts
items.raw_content: Google is launching a new feature for its Phone app that aims to protect you from AI impersonation scams. Now, when you receive a call from a scammer that appears to be coming from the same number as one of your contacts, Phone by Google will flag the call as suspicious so you can hang […]

items.title: Microsoft created the mini Surface dev box that Qualcomm couldn’t
items.raw_content: Microsoft only just announced a new Surface Laptop Ultra at the weekend, and it's now revealing a miniature Surface PC aimed at developers. The new Surface RTX Spark Dev Box is powered by Nvidia's new Arm-based RTX Spark chips, just like the Surface Laptop Ultra, and is optimized for sustained workloads and local AI tasks. […]

items.title: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
items.raw_content: The specification lets developer, compliance, and security teams define their own policies for agents to follow in portable policy files.
items.summary: The specification lets developer, compliance, and security teams define their own policies for agents to follow in portable policy files.

items.title: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in four months
items.raw_content: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.
items.summary: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.

items.title: Martin Scorsese becomes the latest — and most unlikely — Hollywood voice for AI
items.raw_content: The caveat is that one of the world's most famous living directors is using the tech solely for storyboarding.
items.summary: The caveat is that one of the world's most famous living directors is using the tech solely for storyboarding.

items.title: Google rolls out fake call detection to protect against AI deepfake impersonation scams
items.raw_content: As people increasingly refuse to answer calls from unknown numbers, scammers are shifting their tactics by spoofing trusted phone numbers and using AI deepfake technology to sound like authority figures, family members, or employers.

items.title: Amazon faces class action lawsuit over Ring facial-recognition feature
items.raw_content: The class action lawsuit, filed in Seattle by Virginia resident Charles Sigwalt, claims that Ring's Familiar Faces feature stores images of passersby without consent.
items.summary: The class action lawsuit, filed in Seattle by Virginia resident Charles Sigwalt, claims that Ring's Familiar Faces feature stores images of passersby without consent.

items.title: Microsoft Build 2026: All the news about Windows, AI, RTX Spark, and more
items.raw_content: Microsoft Build 2026 kicked off with a keynote presentation that introduced some developer-focused Windows updates, an OpenClaw-based AI assistant called Scout, the new Majorana 2 quantum computing chip, and a Surface mini PC designed for AI developers. Microsoft also rolled out a new Android-based OS called Project Solara that’s designed for gadgets that run AI […]
